concerns.text: Task T025 implements a 'Permutation Test' (10,000 permutations) and explicitly omits the 'Corrected Resampled t-test (Nadeau & Bengio)' mandated by FR-005 for 20 ≤ N < 100. The spec requires BOTH tests to be performed in this range. This is a semantic weakening of the statistical validation requirement, violating the constraint that the deliverable must satisfy the FR it claims to serve.

Task T025 description states: 'Implement Permutation Test... Fallback to Bootstrap 95% CI if N < 20.' It fails to mention the mandatory 'Corrected Resampled t-test' for the 20 ≤ N < 100 range. By omitting this specific test from the task description, the task does not guarantee the implementation of the full statistical suite required by FR-005, creating a risk of silent requirement loss.

Task T025 mentions 'sensitivity analysis sweeping cutoffs {0.01, 0.05, 0.1}' but does not explicitly link this analysis to the Corrected Resampled t-test as required by FR-005. Since the task replaces the t-test with a Permutation Test, the context for this sensitivity analysis is ambiguous and may not satisfy the spec's requirement to assess robustness specifically for the t-test p-value.
